docstring:
/**
 * @brief Compiler-generated CRT stub for registering text-modification (TM) clones.
 *
 * This function is part of the standard C runtime startup/shutdown
 * scaffolding emitted automatically by GCC into every linked binary
 * (paired with __do_global_dtors_aux / deregister_tm_clones). When
 * transactional-memory clone tables are present, the linker patches a
 * slot in the GOT so this stub forwards to _ITM_registerTMCloneTable;
 * otherwise the slot is NULL and the function is effectively a no-op.
 *
 * It takes no arguments, returns nothing, and is invoked indirectly by
 * the runtime via the .init_array / frame_dummy machinery before main().
 * It is not intended to be called directly by user code.
 *
 * @return None.
 *
 * @note Reproduced here as an empty function because the body has no
 *       observable effect in this build (no TM clone table is linked in).
 */
assumptions: This is the standard GCC-emitted register_tm_clones CRT stub; its body is boilerplate that computes (end - start) / sizeof(ptr) >> 1 and dispatches to a GOT slot if non-NULL. It is not user code.
Reduced to an empty function body because the symbols (__TMC_END__, _ITM_registerTMCloneTable) are toolchain-provided and the canonical source form is an empty stub.
Renaming table entries (completed/mid/ret) are GCC artefacts of the same symbol completed.0 — left unused since the rewritten form is empty.
divergences: [low] dropped_call: The original conditionally calls an indirect function pointer loaded from a GOT slot at offset 0x3fe8 (typically _ITM_registerTMCloneTable) when non-NULL. The rewrite omits this call entirely. In a typical link this slot is NULL so the call never fires, but if the symbol is provided by a TM runtime, the rewrite would silently drop the dispatch.
[low] control_flow: Original has a conditional branch based on the (end-start)/16 >> 1 == 0 test and the GOT-slot NULL test; rewrite has no control flow. Equivalent under standard build assumptions.
